name: Neighborgood Partners - Georgetown
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit housing counselor serving Delaware since 1976, offering homeownership education, foreclosure prevention, and financial coaching at no cost.
description_long:
NeighborGood Partners (formerly NCALL) has been a trusted community development organization on the Delmarva Peninsula for nearly 50 years. Founded in 1976, the organization has built expertise in affordable housing, financial education, and community lending to serve low-income families and individuals across Delaware. The Georgetown location is part of their broader service network that addresses housing insecurity and financial vulnerability in rural and underserved regions.

The organization provides comprehensive consumer financial services including HUD-approved pre-purchase homeownership counseling, foreclosure prevention and default counseling, one-on-one financial coaching through their StandByMe partnership, and financial education workshops. They also offer the E-Home America online education course, a Central Delaware Saves matching savings program (1:1 matching for home purchase, education, business startup, or debt payoff), and the Launcher Program for small business development. Beyond consumer services, they operate a certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) loan fund for affordable housing projects and provide technical assistance for self-help housing programs across 21 northeastern states.

NeighborGood Partners distinguishes itself as a mission-driven CDFI with dual focus: direct consumer counseling and systemic community development. Their HUD approval status means their housing counseling meets federal standards. They combine individual financial coaching with neighborhood revitalization initiatives (Restoring Central Dover) and maintain a dedicated loan fund specifically designed to finance affordable housing and community facilities. Their reach extends beyond Delaware into regional self-help housing program support, demonstrating institutional capacity beyond typical local nonprofits.

The organization is best suited for individuals seeking free, legitimate financial counseling rather than quick-fix debt solutions. Consumers should note that while services are free or low-cost, availability may be limited to specific geographic areas and employment/education centers, and matching savings programs have eligibility requirements. The organization's strength is in housing and long-term financial coaching rather than emergency cash assistance.
pros: HUD-approved housing counselor agency with federal credential status
All consumer services appear to be free or low-cost, with no payday loan traps
One-on-one financial coaching available through StandByMe partnership at employment centers
1:1 matching savings program accelerates progress toward multiple goals (homeownership, education, business, debt payoff)
Certified Community Development Financial Institution lending to nonprofits and for-profits for affordable housing
Nearly 50 years of established history (since 1976) serving Delmarva Peninsula
Multi-service approach combining consumer counseling, community development, and lending expertise
cons: Services appear geographically limited to Delaware (Kent and Sussex counties based on contact form); Georgetown location may have limited hours or availability
Website does not clearly specify eligibility requirements, income thresholds, or whether matching savings program has waiting lists
No transparent pricing or timeline information for housing counseling or foreclosure prevention services
E-Home America course requires pre-approval from housing counselor or lender before registration, creating potential barriers
Limited information about response times for inquiries or average timeline to begin counseling services
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking free HUD-approved pre-purchase counseling and guidance through the mortgage process
Homeowners facing foreclosure or default who need non-predatory prevention counseling and application assistance
Individuals with limited financial literacy seeking one-on-one coaching and structured savings programs with matching funds
Small business founders in Dover area needing business startup education through the Launcher Program
diagnosis: NeighborGood Partners Georgetown is ideal for Delaware residents seeking free, legitimate HUD-approved housing counseling or financial coaching without predatory lending or debt settlement pitfalls. The main caveat is that services are geographically limited to Delaware and may have availability constraints; prospective clients should call 302.678.9400 to confirm eligibility and current accessibility at the Georgetown location.
services: HUD-approved homeownership education and pre-purchase housing counseling
Foreclosure prevention and default counseling with application assistance
One-on-one financial coaching through StandByMe partnership at employment centers
E-Home America online education course (pre-registration approval required)
Central Delaware Saves 1:1 matching savings program for home purchase, education, business startup, or debt payoff
CDFI loan fund financing for affordable housing development and community facilities
Technical assistance and consulting for nonprofit affordable housing development
Self-help housing program technical and management support (21-state northeast region)
Restoring Central Dover neighborhood revitalization and community development
The Launcher Program for small business startup education (Dover)
Developer consulting for multi-family affordable housing projects
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
